rationale: Goal r1@ == self_@ && r2@ == self_@ ==> r1 == r2 for CSingleMessage is not provable in Verus. CSingleMessage::Message contains EndPoint (Vec<u8>) and CMessage (Vec<u8>, Option<Vec<u8>>, CKeyHashMap external_body). Verus's spec `==` for external_type_specification Vec and external_body CKeyHashMap is opaque, not derived from view (@) equality (confirmed with a minimal `Vec<u8>` test: `a@ == b@` does NOT yield `a == b`). clone_up_to_view's only ensure is `c@ == self@`, so two calls can legitimately differ structurally while agreeing on view. Partial case-split closes Ack and InvalidMessage variants; the Message variant cannot be closed without an axiom equivalent to assume().
